name: Walt Levinsky
abstract: Walt Levinsky (April 18, 1929 – December 14, 1999) was an American big band and orchestral player, composer, arranger, and bandleader. While many of his big band assignments were as lead alto sax player, his favorite instrument was the clarinet.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Friends of Alec Wilder
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Benny Goodman Revisited: 1993
The New York Pops: Swing With Your Sweetheart
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: And so he’s got Walt Levinsky, myself, a bass player... But we’re doing some pops things
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Imitators, in my opinion, Walt Levinsky and I talk about this all the time
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998
